comments.text: This sequence may be relevant https://oeis.org/A351928
For a detailed description of the problem, one can refer to [La09] by J. C. Lagarias. Edit: This paper [AbLa14] by W. C. Abram and J. C. Lagarias seems related.

As mentioned, the non-averaged version of the main result of "Bits of $3^n$ in binary, Wieferich primes and a conjecture of Erdos(2016)" by T. Dupuy and D. E. Weirich would imply this conjecture. Moreover, they mention that the progress on this conjecture has been done on this one by improving the bounds on the function $N(X)$, where $N(X) =$ {$\# n \leq X : (2^n)_3$ omits a $2$} (For a prime $q$ and a number $N$ we will let $(N)q$ denote the base $q$ expansion of $N$. We view a base $q$ expansion as a string of numbers from the set $\{0, 1, ..., q − 1\}$). The best known bound is due to Narkiewicz in [Na80]: $N(X) \leq 1.62X^{\alpha_0}$, where $\alpha_0= \log_3(2) \approx 0.630$. [Na80]: W. Narkiewicz, A note on a paper of H. Gupta concerning powers of 2 and 3, Publ. Elektroteh. Fak. Univ. Beogr., Ser. Mat. Fiz. 678–715 (1980) 173–174. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
A slight confirmation of this conjecture has been given in this paper by V. S. Dimitrov and E. W. Howe.
In general, every ternary string of digits is expected to appear for all large powers of 2, but we lack methods to decide such questions.
